statement.en: RELATIONS BETWEEN THE ASHTARKHANIDS AND THE OTTOMANS
statement.summary: This article explores the political relations established by representatives of the Ashtarkhanid dynasty with the Ottoman Empire, particularly in the context of their alliance against the Safavids, efforts to reclaim Iraq and Khorasan, and securing the safety of the Hajj pilgrimage route. In addition, the study sheds light on their economic relations in the sphere of trade, as well as cultural interactions involving education and scholarly exchange. The findings demonstrate that these relations were maintained at a highly favorable and constructive level.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a historical scholarship paper (dated 2026, future-dated or preprint status) on Ashtarkhanid-Ottoman relations. Historical claims are empirically grounded but subject to new archival evidence, reinterpretation, and scholarly debate. The abstract presents established historical narrative (alliance against Safavids, trade/cultural exchange) but uses evaluative language ('highly favorable') that requires historiographic consensus validation. Classified FLOWING because it is provisional scholarly work not yet peer-validated in the Rei-AIOS knowledge base cutoff.
